title: Editas Medicine Lays Off 65% of Staff, Shelves Sickle Cell Program
text: Editas Medicine announced it will eliminate approximately 180 positions representing 65% of its workforce and discontinue its flagship gene-editing treatment for sickle cell disease. The CRISPR pioneer, which launched a decade ago amid considerable enthusiasm, has struggled with execution challenges. The company previously conducted layoffs in 2023 as part of restructuring efforts. Its stock declined 81% over the year. These cuts reflect broader difficulties in gene-editing sector where company valuations have contracted significantly and workforce reductions have become commonplace. Editas encountered obstacles pursuing complex, hard-to-reach diseases and experienced substantial leadership turnover as earlier programs stalled or failed to progress. The company indicated it will pursue alternative approaches for treating sickle cell disease rather than continuing with its current gene-editing platform.
